data.title: Hershey jumps amid report of Mondelez takeover approach
data.description:
Hershey (NYSE:HSY) surged 19% after a report that Mondelez (MDLZ) is evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Mondelez fell 2.2%.

Mondelez (MDLZ) made a preliminary approach to Hershey about a possible deal, according to a Bloomberg report on Monday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Deliberations are in the early stages and there’s no certainty talks will lead to a transaction.

Hershey told Bloomberg the company doesn't comment on market rumors. Mondelez didn't respond to requests for comment.

Developing story ...

data.title: Nippon Steel clarifies capex pledge in latest bid to save US Steel deal
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) +2.2% in early trading Monday after Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) reportedly clarified its spending plans for US Steel's American mills, as part of last-ditch efforts to win support for its attempt to buy the company.

After meeting with United Steelworkers leaders, Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) released a letter to US Steel (X) staff including new commitments on where and when a previously announced $1.4B would be spent, Bloomberg reported.

The letter also makes a previously announced additional $1.3B in capital spending legally binding; the funds had been promised after an arbitration meeting, so was not yet a binding proposal.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) is seeking to ease concerns over job security at plants that use traditional blast-furnace production from iron ore as part of its pending $14.1B takeover offer for US Steel (X).

President-elect Trump last week reiterated his opposition to the deal, which was announced a year ago and remains before U.S. regulators.

data.title: Merck reports positive Phase 2 data for zilovertamab vedotin
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) reported positive Phase 2 data from its waveLINE-007 trial evaluating its antibody drug conjugate zilovertamab vedotin in the treatment of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, or DLBCL.

The drug was tested in combination with cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin and prednisone plus rituximab in patients with previously untreated DLBCL. The study showed the zilovertamab vedotin combination achieved a 100% complete response rate in patients treated with zilovertamab vedotin at the 1.75 mg/kg dose, according to a statement.

Merck said that based on the data, the study has established 1.75 mg/kg as the recommended zilovertamab vedotin dose for Phase 3 testing.

The data was presented at the annual meeting of the American Society of Hematology, which is being held Dec. 7-10 in San Diego.

data.title: Reddit rises after releasing test of AI-powered search tool
data.description:
Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) shares moved higher during early market action Monday after the social media company released Reddit Answers, an artificial intelligence-powered search tool.

"With Reddit Answers, redditors can ask questions and receive answers using a new, AI-powered conversational interface," the company said in a blog post today. "Once a question is asked, curated summaries of relevant conversations and details across Reddit will appear, including links to related communities and posts. Redditors can easily read relevant snippets and answers inline from real redditors, jump into the full conversations, and go deeper in their search with their own or suggested follow-up questions."

Reddit Answers is being released as a test version today to a limited number of users in the U.S. It is only available in English in its current form, but will eventually be expanded with more languages and locations.

The new AI-powered search by Reddit sounds similar to Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) AI Overview. Early this year, Reddit entered into a content licensing deal with Google, allowing the search giant to train its AI models on Reddit's content.

Since Reddit became a publicly traded company in March, its stock value has rocketed more than 250%.

data.title: Wholesale inventory growth stays at +0.2% in October's second reading
data.description:
Wholesale inventories rose 0.2% M/M to $90.5.0B in October's second print, the advance estimate of +0.2% and September's -0.2%.

From a year ago, inventories grew 0.9%, the U.S. Census Bureau said on Monday.

data.title: Tesla jumps to three-year high as Optimus humanoid robots step into the spotlight
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) raced to a three-year high of $404.80 in early trading on Monday before settling back.

More analysts are pointing in recent notes to the potential upside from the Optimus business. Bank of America's John Murphy thinks Optimus development poised to accelerate. He noted that Tesla (TSLA) currently plans to have 1K Optimus robots by the end of 2025, largely in its plant. With more robots in use, training is expected to accelerate.

Murphy pointed out that while Optimus accounts for only a single-digit percentage of TSLA’s compute, BofA expects the resources available for Optimus will grow as the robotaxi technology matures. "This will drive an acceleration in the capabilities of Optimus, and ultimately lead to increased production in 2026+, thereby helping drive costs down," he wrote. The Optimus opportunity is seen potentially benefiting from more capital via an equity raise to fund greater compute capacity.

On Seeking Alpha, analyst Steven Fiorillo recently said Tesla (TSLA) is in a prime position to utilize its manufacturing expertise to produce Optimus humanoid robots at a scale that competitors are unlikely to match.

Of note, X Prize Foundation Chairman Peter Diamandis said Tesla's (TSLA) Optimus is set to revolutionize logistics, agriculture, and more.

The latest share price rally for Tesla (TSLA) pushed its market cap over $1.25 trillion.

data.title: Nano Labs prices $36.25M private offering; shares down
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) has entered into a share subscription agreement to sell 5.61M Class A ordinary shares of the company in a private placement priced at $6.46.
  • The aggregate purchase price of the private placement is ~$36.25M. The aggregate purchase price will be satisfied in U.S. dollars, Bitcoin and USDT. 
  • NA shares were down 17.9% on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: PLTR, SMCI, MOMO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Monday as investors eyed this week’s key inflation report. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) data will likely shape expectations for Federal Reserve policy and the U.S. economic outlook.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Hello Group (MOMO) shares rose as much as 7% after the company reported better-than-expected FQ3 results, despite a 12% Y/Y drop in revenue. Looking ahead to FQ4, the company projects total net revenues between RMB2.56B and RMB2.66B, representing a Y/Y decline of 14.7% to 11.4%.
  • Affimed (AFMD) shares surged 8% after the company presented promising data from the LuminICE-203 phase 2 trial at the 66th ASH Annual Meeting. The study, which evaluates the combination of acimtamig (AFM13) and Artiva Biotherapeutics’ allogeneic NK cell product AlloNK® in patients with relapsed/refractory classic Hodgkin lymphoma (R/R cHL), showed a high overall response rate (ORR) of 86% and a complete response rate of 55%. Despite patients being heavily pretreated, the combination therapy demonstrated a well-managed safety profile, with no cases of graft-versus-host disease (GvHD) or neurotoxicity. These results align with previous data from the single-center AFM13-104 study, reinforcing the potential of this combination as an effective treatment option for refractory patients.
  • Shares of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) surged as much as 9% after the company announced that Nasdaq had granted it more time to comply with listing rules. The extension reduces the immediate risk of delisting, as Nasdaq has given Super Micro until February 25 to file its financial report for the fiscal year ended June 30, along with its quarterly report for the period ending September 30. This move provides the company with the breathing room needed to address its reporting delays and maintain its Nasdaq listing.
  • Shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) rose nearly 5% after the U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) expanded its contract with the company, naming Palantir as the lead software integrator for its Mission Command System. The $36.8M, one-year deal marks the first deployment of Palantir’s Mission Manager to Special Operations Forces units, leveraging its secure, Kubernetes-based infrastructure to integrate emerging technologies into a secure environment. The agreement also includes pilot projects aimed at bringing Palantir’s advanced AI capabilities to USSOCOM.
  • Reddit (RDDT) shares climbed 5% after Morgan Stanley upgraded the stock from Equalweight to Overweight and raised its price target significantly from $70 to $200. The stock, which has already surged more than 240% YTD, continues to gain momentum on optimism around Reddit’s growth trajectory. Morgan Stanley cited strong user engagement, effective monetization strategies, and robust EBITDA potential, forecasting a 35% growth in U.S. advertising revenue from 2024 to 2027—outpacing peers. Despite anticipated investments, the firm expects leading incremental margins, with EBITDA projections of $1B in 2026 and $1.4B in 2027. The new price target implies a 23% upside, with a bull case at $300 and a bear case at $105.

Biggest stock losers

  • Shares of BioAge (BIOA) plunged 69% after the company announced Friday it would discontinue its Phase 2 study of the weight-loss drug azelaprag due to safety concerns. The trial, which was testing azelaprag both as a monotherapy and in combination with Eli Lilly's tirzepatide (Mounjaro, Zepbound), was halted after liver transaminitis was observed in a few patients. Of the 204 patients enrolled, 11 in the azelaprag groups showed elevated transaminase levels without clinically significant symptoms. As a result, BioAge will cease dosing and stop enrolling new patients in the trial. The company has informed the FDA of its decision and plans to provide updated information on the drug in Q1 2025.
  • AMD (AMD) shares fell 3% after Bank of America downgraded the semiconductor company to Neutral from Buy, citing weaker projections for AI and PC-related revenue in 2025. Analyst Vivek Arya highlighted increasing competition from Nvidia (NVDA) and the growing preference for custom chips by cloud providers such as Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) as factors limiting AMD’s market share potential. Arya reduced his earnings estimates for 2025 and 2026 by 6% and 8%, respectively, and lowered the price target from $180 to $155. He noted AMD’s pipeline is over a year behind Nvidia’s and lacks a competitive networking portfolio, adding that limited demand for AMD chips, as flagged by Amazon (AMZN), could further weigh on the stock.

data.title: Newmark arranges $1.75B refinancing for two of Fontainebleau Development's Florida resorts
data.description:
• Newmark Group (NASDAQ:NMRK) on Monday said it has arranged $1.2B and $550M loans on behalf of Fontainebleau Development for the refinancing of South Florida resorts Fontainebleau Miami Beach and the JW Marriott Miami Turnberry Resort & Spa, respectively.
  • Newmark secured the financing from Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan on both transactions.
  • NMRK +0.63% premarket to $15.87.
  • Source: Press Release
